commands.repair_reason: Human review should gate merge on real behavior proof for the visible Telegram delivery path; there is no narrow automated code repair to queue from this review.; Cleared: No dependency, workflow, secret, permission, or supply-chain surface changes were introduced; the new path sends a static sanitized notice through an existing internal gateway method. (sha=8704c6c40766ada6178e8cb28f4d903559b7915e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a best-effort visible restart-recovery notice for chat-bound main sessions whose transcript tail cannot be resumed, then keeps marking the stale session failed, with a focused regression test.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main can be exercised with a restart-aborted main session whose transcript tail ends in an assistant message, and the linked issue supplies real Telegram logs for the production symptom. I did not run a live gateway crash/restart reproduction during this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Human review should gate merge on real behavior proof for the visible Telegram delivery path; there is no narrow automated code repair to queue from this review.

target.title: Notify chat when main session restart recovery cannot resume
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #85804 by sending a best-effort visible recovery notice when restart recovery cannot safely resume a chat-bound main session, then marking the stale session failed so retries do not spam.
- Issue proof: a real Telegram group setup hit Codex app-server startup retry exhaustion, typing TTL expiry, and main-session restart recovery failed with `transcript tail is not resumable`, leaving the chat silent/out of context.
- Fix proof: the PR branch's focused recovery shard now validates a Telegram delivery-context session calls `message.action` with the generic "couldn't safely resume" notice before failing the stale session (`17 passed`).

Fixes #85804.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: A Telegram-backed main session could be interrupted by a gateway restart, fail restart recovery with `transcript tail is not resumable`, and leave the chat seeing silence/out-of-context replies. The fixed behavior is a visible generic recovery notice before the stale session is marked failed.

Real environment tested: OpenClaw `2026.5.20` incident from a real Telegram group setup, with a local OpenClaw checkout on this PR branch used for after-fix recovery-path validation.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: From the PR branch checkout, ran `OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=30000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 timeout 75s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-support.config.ts src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts` after adding the recovery-notice path and Telegram delivery-context regression.

Evidence after fix: Terminal capture from the local OpenClaw checkout after the patch:

```text
RUN  v4.1.7 /root/.openclaw/workspace/tmp/openclaw-85804

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  17 passed (17)
Start at  19:22:10
Duration  4.34s (transform 1.83s, setup 344ms, import 3.54s, tests 132ms, environment 0ms)
```

The regression validates the after-fix runtime branch for a Telegram group session with persisted delivery context: recovery calls `message.action` with `action: "send"`, `to`, `threadId`, `accountId`, `sessionKey`, and the generic "couldn't safely resume" notice before marking the interrupted session failed.

Before evidence optional: Redacted runtime log excerpt from the real setup before the fix:

```text
[agent/embedded] codex app-server connection closed during startup; retries exhausted
[agents/harness] Codex agent harness failed; not falling back to embedded PI backend
[diagnostic] lane task error: lane=main durationMs=44080 error="Error: codex app-server client is closed"
Embedded agent failed before reply: codex app-server client is closed
[typing] TTL exceeded (60000ms), auto-stopping typing indicator
[main-session-restart-recovery] marked 1 interrupted main session(s) from stale transcript locks
[main-session-restart-recovery] marked interrupted main session failed: <telegram-main-session> (transcript tail is not resumable)
[main-session-restart-recovery] main-session restart recovery complete: recovered=0 failed=1 skipped=0
```

Observed result after fix: The patched path emits the visible recovery notice call for chat-bound unresumable sessions and still marks the stale interrupted session failed so later restart-recovery retries do not spam the chat.

What was not tested: I did not intentionally crash a live gateway after installing this branch, because that would risk disrupting active sessions and sending confusing live Telegram messages. The live incident evidence is from the real failure; after-fix proof is the focused recovery path validation in the OpenClaw checkout.

## Testing

- `npx oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.ts src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts`
- `npx oxlint src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.ts src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=30000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 timeout 75s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-support.config.ts src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts`

Note: a broad `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --project tsconfig.core.json --pretty false` run hit Node's default heap limit locally. A rerun with `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096` produced no diagnostics before the wrapper timeout bookkeeping lost the exit code, so the focused gates above are the reliable local proof for this PR.
target.author: pfrederiksen
